propositions: Removed env vars are fully silent (not in the alias registry) and fall through to defaults with no warning, while removed TOML keys get only a generic 'unknown key (typo?)' warning that neither names the replacement nor says the key was removed.

A dedicated RemovedInputError, not a subclass of ConfigError, is needed so it propagates past tracker_dir()'s except ConfigError guard instead of silently falling back to the default .tickets-tracker store.

The env-var tombstone check must fire at the top of load_config() before the _RESULT_CACHE lookup (and tombstoned names added to _env_signature) so it is not skipped on a warm cache hit in the long-running MCP server.

Retired inputs affecting store location, write/sync gates, auth, security, or lifecycle policy must hard-error with nonzero exit naming old name, replacement, and removed-in version; operationally inert inputs only warn.
The MCP server must fail a config-load RemovedInputError hard (refuse the write/startup) rather than downgrading it to a normal per-request error while continuing to serve.

A CI check comparing recognised keys/env vars against a committed golden list must fail when a key is removed without a corresponding tombstone entry, keeping the registry complete against future removals.
